title: Acme — Contact a human
purpose: Claude + Playwright capture · AWS Polly narrates (Arthur, en-GB) · ffmpeg stitches

steps.name: home
steps.caption: Landing page
steps.narration: Welcome to Acme — our deliberately boring demo product. A visitor lands on the home page, and the primary call to action is right up front: Get in touch.

steps.name: contact-open
steps.caption: Contact form opened
steps.narration: Clicking Get in touch routes the visitor to a simple contact form. Three fields — name, email, and message — and a single submit button. Nothing clever.

steps.name: validation
steps.caption: Inline validation
steps.narration: Submitting an empty form surfaces inline validation against each field. No malformed data ever reaches the back end — the user sees exactly what is missing.

steps.name: filled
steps.caption: Form completed
steps.narration: Once the fields are filled in with valid input the inline errors disappear automatically, and the form is ready to send.

steps.name: ready-to-send
steps.caption: Ready to send
steps.narration: With every field valid, the Send message button is now armed. One click submits the message to the back end.

steps.name: success
steps.caption: Submission confirmed
steps.narration: The POST to slash A P I slash contact succeeds, and the user lands on a confirmation page with a unique ticket identifier.

steps.name: admin
steps.caption: Appears in submissions
steps.narration: And here is the end-to-end proof. The submission shows up in the back office list with a fresh timestamp — exactly the row Ada just created.
